content.body: A force is a push or a pull on an object. Forces can make objects start moving, stop moving, speed up, slow down, or change direction. Forces can also change the shape of an object.
Force is measured in Newtons (N). Named after Sir Isaac Newton.

There are many types of forces: Gravity pulls objects downward toward the Earth. Friction opposes motion between two surfaces. Normal force acts perpendicular to a surface. Applied force is a push or pull by a person or machine. Tension acts through ropes and strings.

content.title: Forces in Daily Life
content.body:
When you kick a football:
• Your foot applies a force on the ball (applied force)
• The ball moves in the direction of the force
• Eventually friction and air resistance slow it down
• Gravity pulls it back to the ground

Newton's Second Law: Force = Mass × Acceleration
F = ma
Where F is in Newtons (N), m is in kilograms (kg), a is in metres per second squared (m/s²)

content.title: Calculating Force
content.body:
A box of mass 5 kg accelerates at 3 m/s². What force acts on it?
F = ma = 5 × 3 = 15 N

questions.question: What is a force?
options: A push or pull on an object
questions.answer: A push or pull on an object
questions.explanation: A force is defined as a push or pull acting on an object.

questions.question: What unit is force measured in?
questions.explanation: Force is measured in Newtons (N), named after Sir Isaac Newton.

questions.question: A 10 kg object accelerates at 2 m/s². What force acts on it?
Which force always pulls objects toward the Earth?
questions.explanation: Gravity is the force that pulls all objects toward the centre of the Earth.

questions.question: What does friction do to moving objects?
questions.explanation: Friction is a force that opposes motion between two surfaces in contact.

questions.question: If F = 30 N and m = 6 kg, what is the acceleration?
What happens to an object when a net force acts on it?
questions.explanation: A net force causes an object to accelerate (change its velocity).

questions.question: Which force acts through a rope?
questions.explanation: Tension is the force transmitted through a rope, string or cable.

questions.question: What does Newton's Second Law state?
questions.explanation: Newton's Second Law: Force equals mass multiplied by acceleration (F = ma).

questions.question: A force of 24 N acts on a 8 kg mass. What is the acceleration?

content.body: Speed is how fast an object moves — it tells us the distance covered per unit of time. Velocity is speed in a specific direction. Acceleration is the rate at which velocity changes.

Speed = Distance ÷ Time
v = d/t
Unit: metres per second (m/s)

Acceleration = Change in Velocity ÷ Time
a = (v − u) ÷ t
Where v = final velocity, u = initial velocity, t = time
Unit: m/s²

content.title: Calculating Speed
content.body:
A car travels 120 m in 8 seconds. What is its speed?
v = d/t = 120/8 = 15 m/s

content.title: Calculating Acceleration
content.body:
A motorbike goes from 0 m/s to 20 m/s in 5 seconds. What is its acceleration?
a = (v − u)/t = (20 − 0)/5 = 4 m/s²
Deceleration is negative acceleration — when an object slows down. If a car goes from 30 m/s to 10 m/s in 4 seconds: a = (10 − 30)/4 = −5 m/s² (the negative sign shows deceleration).

questions.question: What is the formula for speed?
questions.explanation: Speed = Distance ÷ Time (v = d/t)

questions.question: A student runs 200 m in 25 seconds. What is their speed?
What is the unit of acceleration?
questions.explanation: Acceleration is measured in metres per second squared (m/s²).

questions.question: A car accelerates from 10 m/s to 30 m/s in 5 s. What is its acceleration?
How is velocity different from speed?
questions.explanation: Velocity is speed in a specific direction. Speed has no direction.

questions.question: A bus travels at 20 m/s for 60 seconds. How far does it travel?
What does a negative acceleration mean?
questions.explanation: Negative acceleration means the object is slowing down (decelerating).

questions.question: A boda boda goes from 15 m/s to 0 m/s in 3 seconds. What is the deceleration?
questions.explanation: a = (0−15)/3 = −15/3 = −5 m/s² (negative = deceleration)

questions.question: If a car travels at 25 m/s, how long does it take to cover 500 m?
An object starts at rest and reaches 12 m/s in 4 seconds. What is its acceleration?
